name: dsock

metadata.name: dsock
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v2 (GPLv2)

documentation:
# dsock

dsock is a bi-directional, multiplexing socket server that acts as a proxy between two machines
sharing a common file system. This is useful in situations where there is no direct network
connectivity. For example, dsock would enable connectivity to a service over a Windows remote
desktop session.

## Quick Start

There are no dependencies other than Python 3.12. Clone the repository and update the configuration
dictionary in `server.py`. The library package can be installed in site-packages although it isn't
necessary for running the server.

As an example scenario, connecting to machine A's port 8080 will reach machine B's port 80:

```python
config = {
    'a': {'pipes': [], 'tcp-sockets': [('127.0.0.1', 8080, '127.0.0.1', 80, '/mnt/share/web.sock')]},
    'b': {'pipes': ['/mnt/share/web.sock'], 'tcp-sockets': []}}
```

Then start the server on A:

```
$ python server.py a
```

And also on B:

```
$ python server.py b
```

functions.name: dsock.channel.nop
documentation: No operation coroutine.

methods.name: dsock.channel.Channel.__init__
documentation: Bi-directional communication channel between two endpoints.

methods.name: dsock.channel.Channel.number
documentation: Returns the channel number.

methods.name: dsock.channel.Channel.address
documentation: Returns the remote IP address. This is only used when opening a channel.

methods.name: dsock.channel.Channel.port
documentation: Returns the remote IP port. This is only used when opening a channel.

methods.name: dsock.channel.Channel.ready
documentation: Returns the channel ready event.

methods.name: dsock.channel.Channel.is_datagram
documentation: Returns whether the channel handles datagram packets.

methods.name: dsock.channel.Channel.is_open
documentation: Returns whether the channel is open.

methods.name: dsock.channel.Channel.next_sequence
documentation: Returns the next segment sequence number.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Flags.__init__
documentation: Bitfield for transmission options.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Flags.encode
documentation: Encodes the flags into a single byte.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Flags.decode
documentation: Decodes the flags from a single byte.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Header.__init__
documentation: Segment header containing transmission metadata.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Header._encode_addr
documentation: Encodes the IP address into bytes.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Header._decode_addr
documentation: Decodes the IP address from bytes.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Header.encode
documentation: Encodes the header into bytes.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Header.decode
documentation: Decodes the header from bytes.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Segment.__init__
documentation: Byte bundle containing the metadata header and payload.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Segment.header
documentation: Returns the segment header.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Segment.data
documentation: Returns the segment payload.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Segment.total_size
documentation: Returns the total expected size of the segment.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Segment.decode
documentation: Decodes the segment from bytes.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Segment.__len__
documentation: Returns the total number of bytes in the segment.

methods.name: dsock.transport.Segment.__repr__
documentation: Returns a string representation of the segment.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Frame.__init__
documentation: A byte blob with metadata.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Frame.data
documentation: Returns the payload bytes.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Frame.__len__
documentation: Returns the payload byte count.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Frame.encode
documentation: Converts the frame to a byte array.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Frame.decode
documentation: Converts a byte array to a frame.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Pipe.__init__
documentation: A file-based pipe for remote communication.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Pipe.allocate
documentation: Allocates the pipe's buffer files.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Pipe.open_reader
documentation: Opens a handle to the read buffer.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Pipe.open_writer
documentation: Opens a handle to the write buffer.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Pipe.read
documentation: Read data from the pipe.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Pipe._truncate
documentation: Sets the write control byte to NULL, effectively emptying the buffer.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Pipe._wait_write
documentation:
Waits for the write control byte to be set to NULL. This indicates that the
receiving process has read the data from the buffer.

methods.name: dsock.pipe.Pipe.write
documentation: Writes data to the pipe.

class_variables.name: dsock.pipe.Pipe.BUFFER_SIZE
documentation: 4MB

functions.name: dsock.datagram_channel_open
documentation: Callback invoked when a remote channel is opened.

functions.name: dsock.datagram_channel_close
documentation: Callback invoked when a remote channel is closed.

functions.name: dsock.stream_channel_wait
documentation: Closes the channel when the reader is at EOF.

functions.name: dsock.stream_channel_open
documentation: Callback invoked when a remote channel is opened.

functions.name: dsock.stream_channel_close
documentation: Callback invoked when a remote channel is closed.

functions.name: dsock.stream_channel_data_received
documentation: Callback invoked when data is received on a channel.

functions.name: dsock.stream_data_loop
documentation: Continuously reads data from the stream reader and sends it over the channel.

functions.name: dsock.stream_connection_accepted
documentation: Callback invoked when a connection is accepted.

functions.name: dsock.channel_open
documentation: Callback invoked when a remote channel is opened.

functions.name: dsock.channel_close
documentation: Callback invoked when a remote channel is closed.

functions.name: dsock.create_pipe_socket
documentation: Creates a new pipe socket.

functions.name: dsock.start_pipe_server
documentation: Starts a server that listens for incoming pipe connections.

functions.name: dsock.start_stream_listener
documentation: Starts a stream server that listens for incoming connections.

functions.name: dsock.start_datagram_listener
documentation: Starts a datagram server that listens for incoming messages.

methods.name: dsock.DatagramProtocol.__init__
documentation: A datagram forwarder for pipe sockets.

methods.name: dsock.DatagramProtocol.connection_made
documentation: Callback for when the datagram endpoint is created.

methods.name: dsock.DatagramProtocol.connection_lost
documentation: Callback for when the datagram endpoint closes.

methods.name: dsock.DatagramProtocol.datagram_received
documentation: Callback for when a datagram is received.

methods.name: dsock.DatagramProtocol.channel_data_received
documentation: Callback for when data data is received on the channel.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.Packet.__init__
documentation: Transmission unit containing a channel and segment.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.Packet.channel
documentation: Returns the channel associated with the packet.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.Packet.segment
documentation: Returns the segment contained in the packet.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.Packet.is_setup
documentation: Returns whether the packet contains a channel setup segment.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.Packet.is_reset
documentation: Returns whether the packet contains a channel reset segment.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.Packet.is_refused
documentation: Returns whether the packet contains a channel refused segment.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.Packet.is_data
documentation: Returns whether the packet contains a data segment.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.Packet.is_fin
documentation: Returns whether the packet contains a final data segment.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.Packet.__repr__
documentation: Returns a string representation of the packet.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.Packet.__eq__
documentation: Compares the channel and sequence number of two packets.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.__init__
documentation: Bi-directional communication protocol for managing channels and packets.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.packet_size
documentation: Returns the maximum packet size.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.allocate_channel
documentation: Allocates a new channel number.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.free_channel
documentation: Deallocates a channel number.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.close_channel
documentation: Closes the channel and prepares a reset packet.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.open_channel
documentation: Opens a new channel and prepares a setup packet.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.channel_setup
documentation: Acknowledges the setup packet and marks the channel as open.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.channel_refused
documentation: Refuses channel setup and frees the channel number.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.channel_reset
documentation: Acknowledges the reset packet and marks the channel as closed.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.channel_exists
documentation: Checks if the channel number in the packet exists.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.send
documentation: Sends a packet to the remote endpoint.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.recv
documentation: Receives a packet from the remote endpoint.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.unpack
documentation: Unpacks the data segment and forwards it to the channel.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol._chunk
documentation: Splits the data into chunks of the maximum packet payload size.

methods.name: dsock.protocol.PacketProtocol.pack
documentation: Packs the data into segments and prepares them for sending.

methods.name: dsock.socket.TransmitQueue.__init__
documentation: A thread-safe queue for transmitting packets.

methods.name: dsock.socket.TransmitQueue.__iter__
documentation: Returns an iterator for the queue.

methods.name: dsock.socket.TransmitQueue.__len__
documentation: Returns the number of packets in the queue.

methods.name: dsock.socket.TransmitQueue.append
documentation: Appends a packet to the queue.

methods.name: dsock.socket.TransmitQueue.insert
documentation: Inserts a packet at the beginning of the queue.

methods.name: dsock.socket.TransmitQueue.peek
documentation: Returns the first packet in the queue without removing it.

methods.name: dsock.socket.TransmitQueue.pop
documentation: Removes and return the packet at the specified index.

methods.name: dsock.socket.TransmitQueue.drop
documentation: Removes packets from the queue that match the given predicate.

methods.name: dsock.socket.TransmitQueue.lock
documentation: Returns the lock object for the queue.

methods.name: dsock.socket.PipeSocket.__init__
documentation: A socket-like interface for sending and receiving data over a pipe.

methods.name: dsock.socket.PipeSocket.start
documentation: Starts the socket's send and receive loops.

methods.name: dsock.socket.PipeSocket.open_channel
documentation: Opens a channel to the specified address and port.

methods.name: dsock.socket.PipeSocket.close_channel
documentation: Closes the specified channel.

methods.name: dsock.socket.PipeSocket.send
documentation: Sends data over the specified channel.

methods.name: dsock.socket.PipeSocket._send_loop
documentation: Continuously sends packets from the queue.

methods.name: dsock.socket.PipeSocket._cancel_refused_channel
documentation: Cancels a channel opening that was refused by the remote peer.

methods.name: dsock.socket.PipeSocket._on_remote_open
documentation: Handles a channel open event from the remote peer.

methods.name: dsock.socket.PipeSocket._on_remote_close
documentation: Handles a channel close event from the remote peer.

methods.name: dsock.socket.PipeSocket._on_data_received
documentation: Handles a data received event from the remote peer.

methods.name: dsock.socket.PipeSocket._recv_loop
documentation: Continuously receives packets from the pipe.

methods.name: dsock.socket.SocketChannel.__init__
documentation: Convenience wrapper for a channel object with send and close methods.

methods.name: dsock.socket.SocketChannel.number
documentation: Returns the channel number.

methods.name: dsock.socket.SocketChannel.address
documentation: Returns the channel IP address.

methods.name: dsock.socket.SocketChannel.port
documentation: Returns the channel port number.

methods.name: dsock.socket.SocketChannel.on_data_received
documentation: A callback that is invoked when data is received on the channel.

methods.name: dsock.socket.SocketChannel.on_data_received
documentation: Sets the callback that is invoked when data is received on the channel.

methods.name: dsock.socket.SocketChannel.is_open
documentation: Returns whether the channel is open.

methods.name: dsock.socket.SocketChannel.is_datagram
documentation: Returns whether the channel handles datagram packets.

methods.name: dsock.socket.SocketChannel.send
documentation: Sends data over the channel.

methods.name: dsock.socket.SocketChannel.close
documentation: Closes the channel.
